title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter for protocol outcomes, layers, standards, and evidence. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.
Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.
Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 1859 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Outcome Evidence Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Outcome Evidence Contract, keep Outcome Evidence Contract connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
